title: ConvertKit vs AWeber (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: ConvertKit vs AWeber: compare pricing, features, and real use cases to find the right email tool for your needs.
intro: Choosing between ConvertKit and AWeber comes down to your specific use case. Both are legitimate email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. ConvertKit is built for creators, bloggers, and course sellers, while AWeber targets bloggers and solopreneurs wanting simplicity. This comparison covers the key differences so you can decide quickly.
choose_a_if: You are primarily focused on creators, bloggers, and course sellers
choose_b_if: You are primarily focused on bloggers and solopreneurs wanting simplicity

faq.a: It depends on your use case. ConvertKit wins for creators, bloggers, and course sellers, while AWeber is stronger for bloggers and solopreneurs wanting simplicity. Neither is objectively better — the right choice depends on your specific needs.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ConvertKit or AWeber?
faq.a: AWeber starts at a lower price point ($15/mo vs $29/mo). However, pricing scales with subscriber count, so the total cost depends on your list size.

faq.q: Can I switch from AWeber to ConvertKit?
faq.a: Yes, switching from AWeber to ConvertKit is possible. Most platforms support CSV import/export for subscriber lists. Plan for some time to recreate automations and templates, as these rarely transfer directly between platforms.
